what_it_does: Declares a single RELATION_TYPE dict for a 'reply_to' board edge: a directed edge from a reply board item to the comment/note board item it replies to, with inverse 'has_reply', label 'reply-to', and a descriptive docstring explaining it builds comment threads.
observations: Module-level constant RELATION_TYPE only; no functions or classes.
Fields: id, directed (True), label, inverse ('has_reply'), desc.
desc encodes usage: reply_to points to board://<comment-id>, and reverse_traverse(comment, reply_to) gathers replies, nested recursively.
desc names the threading model: comment links commented_on -> address; reply links reply_to -> that comment.
Attributes the feature to Tim ('the replied-to comments Tim named') and frames annotation as a conversation, not a flat list.
standouts: Has an explicit inverse ('has_reply'), unlike the two sibling files in this batch.
Describes recursive nesting via reverse_traverse as the threading mechanism.
conventions: Follows the board_edges relation-type declaration pattern: one RELATION_TYPE dict per file.
directed=True with a meaningful inverse aligns with the repo's directional-edge law.
notes: A pure data declaration of the reply threading edge; the logic lives in whatever consumes RELATION_TYPE, not here.
questions: Where is RELATION_TYPE registered/consumed (what reads board_edges/*.py)?
Is reverse_traverse a real helper referenced elsewhere or just descriptive prose?
purpose_vs_actual: Match: the file's stated purpose (declare the reply-to threading edge) is exactly what its single dict does.
intention_signals: Aims to make comment threading a first-class, traversable graph edge so the linking layer parses replies into nested threads rather than treating them as flat references.
novelty: Encodes a recursive comment-thread structure entirely as a relation-type declaration with an inverse for reverse traversal.
contribution: Contributes the reply-to threading edge definition and its inverse, enabling nested comment-thread traversal on the board.
summary_for_embedding: A board_edges relation-type declaration defining the directed reply_to edge (inverse has_reply) that links a reply board item to the comment it replies to, building nested comment threads.
intention_for_embedding: To make comment-reply threading a first-class traversable graph edge so annotations form conversational threads rather than a flat list.
